trivia_bullets.text: Common, THB original print only. Type line is Creature — Cyclops (not enchantment creature), placing it as a mortal-world creature, not Nyxborn. Cyclopes on Theros are wild wilderness inhabitants, not polis citizens.
sources: MTG Wiki: Theros plane via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: "A cyclops thinks slowly but strikes quickly. Try to keep it thinking." — Brigone, soldier of Meletis. Brigone is an unnamed-lore figure. Meletis is Theros's philosopher-polis associated with blue mana and Ephara, god of the polis — the red monster/blue city tension is embedded in the attribution.
sources: MTG Wiki: Meletis via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: The ability (can't block when two or more creatures attack) is a direct flavor-mechanical fusion: the cyclops cannot handle processing multiple threats simultaneously. Art by Mark Behm. Hipsters of the Coast common review grade C.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: THB Common Review 2020-01

trivia_bullets.text: 4/4 Reach for 4 mana is above-rate for a common, but the blocking gap against wide attacks is a real exploit. Legal in Pauper and Pauper Commander.

related_cards.name: Hundred-Handed One
related_cards.note: Contrasting Greek-myth giant from original Theros; opposite blocking problem

related_cards.name: Nyxborn Brute
related_cards.note: Companion cyclops common in same set — Nyxborn variant, contrasting flavor
